title: Flaky Fried Syrian Lamb Pilaf
description: A spicy and flaky Syrian dish featuring ground clove and bonito flakes, cooked to perfection in a claypot. This recipe is onion and garlic free, making it suitable for those with dietary restrictions.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Rinse the rice in a fine mesh sieve until the water runs clear.
In a large claypot, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the ground lamb and cook until it is browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the ground clove, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the bonito flakes and stir to combine.
Add the rice to the claypot and stir to combine with the lamb mixture.
Add the water and lemon juice to the claypot, and bring to a boil.
Cover the claypot with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until the rice is tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
Remove the claypot from the oven and sprinkle the chopped parsley over the top.
